issues.title: Cross-feature import: products feature imports from cart feature
issues.description: MobileBarWithCart.tsx (inside features/products) directly imports `useAddToCart` from `@/features/cart`. Features must not import directly from other features. Cross-feature communication should go through shared hooks in `/shared/application/` or be composed at the page/app level.
issues.fix: Move the composition of the cart hook and product display up to the page or a shared integration component. Alternatively, place `useAddToCart` in `@/shared/application/hooks/` if it is truly shared, and have both features depend on the shared abstraction.

issues.title: Hardcoded magic string 'stock_error' without a constant
issues.description: The string literal `"stock_error"` is thrown as an error message in infrastructure and then compared in two presentation components (SellerCheckoutContent.tsx:83 and SellerCheckoutCard.tsx:159). There is no named constant for this sentinel value in the checkout domain constants file. A typo in any one location would silently break the stock-error UI path.
issues.fix: Add `export const STOCK_ERROR_CODE = "stock_error";` to `apps/payments/src/features/checkout/domain/constants.ts` and replace the three raw string literals with the constant.

issues.title: Hardcoded session-storage value sentinel "1" without a constant
issues.description: The value `"1"` used to flag checkout completion in sessionStorage is a magic string. It is set at line 29 and compared at line 21. While the key itself is properly extracted to `CHECKOUT_COMPLETED_SESSION_KEY`, the value is not.
issues.fix: Add `export const CHECKOUT_COMPLETED_VALUE = "1";` to the checkout domain constants and use it in both the set and get calls.

issues.title: Internal render helper function returns JSX inside component
issues.description: `renderContent()` is defined as a named function inside `SellerReportsPage` and returns JSX. While it does not call hooks (so it avoids the stale-closure pitfall), extracting loading/error/data states into a standalone component is the preferred pattern (see component-patterns.md). This is a minor style concern and does not cause a runtime issue.
issues.fix: Consider extracting the conditional content to `<SellerReportsContent orders={orders} total={total} isLoading={isLoading} isError={isError} />` as a separate component or just inline the conditional JSX directly in the render return.

issues.title: Missing barrel index.ts files for feature layers across multiple apps
issues.description: The architecture rule requires each feature layer (domain, application, infrastructure, presentation) to have an `index.ts` exporting its public API. None of the layer subdirectories in features/reports, features/checkout, features/assigned-orders, features/orders, features/payment-methods, features/received-orders (payments), nor in store features/auth, store features/cart, store features/products layers have these barrel files. This means consumers must import deep internal paths instead of layer-level public APIs.
issues.fix: Add `index.ts` to each feature layer directory, re-exporting the intended public surface. This is a widespread pattern inconsistency across all apps; prioritize the most-imported layers first (e.g. checkout/domain, checkout/application).

issues.title: app-components package uses next-intl directly (by design)
issues.description: AppNavigation, LocaleSwitcher, ThemeToggle, EmptyState, ErrorState, LoadingState, and ExportDropdown in packages/app-components all call `useTranslations` from next-intl. The standard rule for packages is no i18n (props injection instead). However, CLAUDE.md explicitly annotates this package as '# App-level shared components (uses next-intl)', indicating this is a deliberate architectural decision — app-components is a shared-but-i18n-aware layer. No action required, but future components added to this package should follow the same documented exception.
issues.fix: No fix needed — this is intentional per CLAUDE.md. Document the exception for new contributors.

issues.title: Hardcoded production URL in seed script
issues.description: `IMG_BASE` is hardcoded to `https://moonfest.furrycolombia.com` and the final console.log references `https://store.furrycolombia.com/store`. Seed scripts are DevOps tooling and this is acceptable — they are not production application code. Noted for awareness only.
issues.fix: Optional: accept IMG_BASE and STORE_URL as environment variables for flexibility across environments. Not required.
